title: rhynchosia procurrens subsp. latisepala
intro: Rhynchosia procurrens subsp. latisepala is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). It is a climber with pink or purple flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is known to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various infections. In herbal preparations, Rhynchosia procurrens subsp. latisepala is often made into a tea or infused oil, and can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin irritations.
